Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 241 [Maqlu Varia][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] x [x x] x x [x] x [x x] ⸢TI⸣

(o 1) (1 line fragmentary)

o 22

[...] ⸢dNIN⸣—[E₂].AN.⸢NA⸣ [x] ⸢šu⸣-bat ilu-ti-šu₂

(o 2) [...] Belet-ayyaki [...] her divine dwelling,

o 33

[... e-piš-ti]-⸢ia₅⸣ u ⸢muš-te-piš⸣-ti-ia₅ ana giSAG.KU₅ GARan KU₄ub

(o 3) he sets up [...] of my bewitcher and my enchantress for a tube and enters.

o 44

[...] NIG₂? ina-an-na-ma U₄ 30?-KAM

(o 4) [...] ... now and on the 30th day,

o 55

[x x] LA NIG₂? d30 ša₂ [x] ⸢DUNGU?⸣-MEŠ dUTU ša₂ lib₃-bi ⸢an⸣-qul-lu₄

(o 5) [...] ... Sin of [...] clouds and Šamaš of fiery glow,

o 66

[x d]NIN—E₂.AN.NA [...] ša₂ šu-⸢bat⸣ x [...] ilu-⸢ti-šu₂⸣

(o 6) [...] Belet-ayyaki [...] of her divine [...] dwelling,

o 77

[x] ša₂ NU-ME e-piš-ti-ia₅ u muš-te-piš-ti-ia₅ ana giSAG.KU₅ MAŠ ⸢GI?⸣ x LUM

(o 7) [...] the figurines of my bewitcher and my enchantress ... for a tube.

o 88

⸢e?⸣-te-rid? KA? e-te-x-a ina a-mat d30 e-⸢te⸣-lil!

(o 8) I went down. I .... I became pure at the word of Sin.

o 99

ina a-mat dUTU e-te-⸢bi-ib⸣ [x a]-⸢mat⸣ dNIN—E₂.AN.NA el-le-⸢ku eb⸣-be₂-⸢ku⸣

(o 9) I became cleansed at the word of Šamaš. I am pure and I am cleansed (at) the word of Belet-ayyaki.

o 1010

[x] x ŠU U x [...] ⸢munus⸣UŠ₁₁.ZU-ia₅ x x x DIR? x x x ZU?

(o 10) [...] ... [...] of my spell-caster ....

o 1111

u₄-mu ITI MU.AN.⸢NA⸣ [...] ⸢4?⸣-KAM? u₄?-⸢mu?⸣ x [...] x x-⸢KAM?⸣

(o 11) [...] day, month and year ... [...] ...,

o 1212

[x x] IGI KI? ŠUB? [x] BA x [...] munus⸢UŠ₁₁⸣.[ZU ...] x BA?

(o 12) [...] ... [...] ... [...] the spell-caster [...] ....

o 1313

[...] ṣab-tu ana? BAD? ⸢IL?⸣ x x a-a i-[...] x-⸢šu₂⸣ lim-ḫaṣ

(o 13) [...] seized ... not [...]. Let him beat his ....

o 1414

[...] TAR LUḪ? KIti₃ x x DU-šu₂ x NAM? NIG₂ LUḪ ⸢KIti₃⸣ ṭe₃-en-šu₂ liš-bu-u₂

(o 14) [...] .... Let them ... the earth's ... and the earth's ....

o 1515

[...] ⸢d⸣30 [...] ša₂ munusUŠ₁₁.ZU-ia₅ ZI-šu₂ ḫi₂-pi₂<(lib)>-li-ma

(o 15) [...] Sin [...]. May my spell-caster's life (gloss: break) come to an end.

o 1616

[...] U? [...] U x x UŠ? IGI ana KUR.NU.GI₄

(o 16) [...] ... to the Land Of No Return (the Underworld).

o 1717

[...] x IM x GARan ZA NU E₂ ana giSAG.KU₅

(o 17) He sets up [...] ..., ... for the tube,

o 1818

[...] 29? x 30

(o 18) [...] 29 ... 30,

o 1919

[...] NU ana ŠA₃ x [...]

(o 19) [...] the figurines into ... [...].

o 2020

[...] x [...]

(o 20) (1 line fragmentary)

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x [...]

(r 1') (1 line fragmentary)

r 2'2'

[...] MU E TAR ⸢ŠU?⸣ d30 LUL? x [x x]

(r 2') [...] ... Sin ... [...].

r 3'3'

[...] GARan KAŠ SAG BALqi₂ KA₂ giSAG.KU₅

(r 3') You set up [...]. You pour out best beer. The opening of the tube [...] .... You recite the incantation three times overhead.

r 4'4'

[x x] A TE du-un-na TU? TAR UGU MA EN₂ 3-šu₂ ina UGU ŠIDnu

r 5'5'

[x] x TAR? MA ana giSAG.KU₅ GARan ša₂ giSAG.KU₅ ba-bu-ša₂ te-pe!-eḫ-ḫe-e-ma

(r 5') You set up ... [...] for the tube. You seal the tube's opening, and then you throw it into water, and then the spells are released.

r 6'6'

a-na ŠA₃ A-MEŠ ŠUBdi-ma kiš-pi pa-aš₂-ru

(catchline)
r 7'7'

EN₂ an-nu-u₂ bi-ib-lu an-nu-u₂ bi-ib-bu-lu₄

(r 7') Incantation: 'This, the (moon's) removal, this, the disappearance.'

(colophon)
r 8'8'

GABA.RI IM ša₂ TA UGU IM SUMUN ⸢MEŠ?⸣.LAM ša₂-ṭir-ma IGI.KAR₂ AK.A

(r 8') Copy of a tablet, written after an original tablet of Mešlam, and then checked and finished.

r 9'9'

⸢IM⸣ mda-nu—ik-ṣu-ur gi-nu!-u₂ mdša-maš₂—SUMna ŠA₃.BA₃.BA₃.ALLA

(r 9') Tablet of Anu-ikṣur, the son of Šamaš-iddina, descendant of Šangi-Ninurta, the mašmaššu-priest. He who reveres Anšar and Kišar shall not take (the tablet) away.

r 10'10'

[lu₂KID.BAR]—dLUGAL.URU.BAR.RA lu₂MAŠ.MAŠši MUD AN.ŠAR₂ u₃ dKI.ŠAR₂ la₃ TUM₃!
